frameworks.name: invariance learning for concept generalisation
frameworks.one_line: rotational invariance over transition vectors enables abstract concept recognition

frameworks.name: spatial scaffolding of abstract cognition
frameworks.one_line: physical space provides a transferable structure for non-spatial relational learning

frameworks.name: relational structure learning
frameworks.one_line: concepts as transition patterns on a two-dimensional feature manifold

core_question: How do humans learn invariances that allow generalisation of abstract relational concepts across rotations?
key_assumptions: concepts can be defined as transition patterns on a 2D manifold
feature manifolds and physical space can be operationalised analogously
manipulations: domain of stimulus sequences (physical space vs nonspatial feature manifold)
rotation of transition vectors across exemplars
pre-training that maps auditory or visual features onto spatial locations
key_findings: humans generalise rotated exemplars in physical space but not on nonspatial feature manifolds
feature-to-space pre-training enables rotational generalisation in nonspatial domains
rotational invariance is not automatic for abstract concepts
interpretations: space acts as a scaffold for learning abstract conceptual invariances
spatial mappings can transfer rotational invariance to nonspatial concept domains
